text.title: Continuous Discovery Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Continuous Discovery application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Discovery MUST run ≥1 customer touchpoint per week (≥30 min); biweekly or ad-hoc rejected.
rule.rationale: Below weekly cadence, recall fades and the next conversation re-derives same questions.

rule.statement: Discovery MUST maintain an opportunity-solution-tree rooted in ≥1 named outcome; flat opportunity lists rejected.
rule.rationale: Tree structure forces tracing every solution back to its outcome and assumption.

rule.statement: Every opportunity MUST carry ≥1 falsifiable assumption test in the queue; opportunities without tests are parked, not built.
rule.rationale: Without assumption tests, opportunities collapse to favourite-feature lists.

rule.statement: Every solution promoted to backlog MUST cite the touchpoint(s) + assumption test that supported promotion; un-cited solutions are rejected.
rule.rationale: Cited promotions survive retroactive review; un-cited collapse to opinion.

rule.statement: Tree + queue + log carry version + last_reviewed.
